steps.title: Support the comparison
steps.content: The passage claims that peaking hydroelectric plants can generate more megawatt-hours of power per year than some run-of-river plants, even though peaking plants do not typically operate continuously. The blank must complete the example by using the table's mode and average annual power generation columns to compare a peaking plant with run-of-river plants.

steps.title: Use the relevant values
steps.content: The table shows that Great Falls is a peaking plant with average annual power generation of 124,392 MWh/yr. The two run-of-river plants listed are Scanlon, at 7,511 MWh/yr, and Kansas River, at 15,345 MWh/yr. Great Falls therefore generates more average annual power than both run-of-river plants in the table.

steps.title: Eliminate mismatches
steps.content:
• Choice B refers to generator counts, but the table gives no generator-count data.
  • Choice C says the top run-of-river plant exceeds the top peaking plant, which contradicts the table.
  • Choice D compares two run-of-river plants, so it does not support a peaking-versus-run-of-river claim.

steps.title: Choose the direct example
steps.content: Choice A directly states that Great Falls generates more average annual power than any run-of-river plant in the table. That is exactly the example needed to support the passage's claim. The answer is A.
